mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in >65% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 5-day returns in >60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 275 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 275.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, providing immediate upward pressure on equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while total payrolls declines signals labor market decoupling and structural weakness, leading to negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.
